@@ -58,7 +58,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-545","title":"Integrate preflight into install.sh as optional first step","description":"# Task: Integrate preflight into install.sh as optional first step ## Context Part of EPIC: Pre-Flight Validation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0ok) ## Rationale Users who skip the wizard and run the installer directly should still get pre-flight validation. Currently they only see failures 15 minutes in. ## Proposal Add preflight as Phase 0 of install.sh: ```bash run_phase 0 "Pre-Flight Validation" run_preflight_checks ``` ### Behavior - Run all preflight checks - On FAIL: Show errors, exit (don't waste time) - On WARN: Show warnings, ask to continue (unless --yes) - On PASS: Continue silently ### Flag ```bash --skip-preflight # Skip pre-flight checks (for debugging) ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Preflight runs before Phase 1 - Failures abort immediately with actionable message - Warnings shown but don't block (with --yes) - --skip-preflight available for edge cases ## Files to Modify - install.sh: Add Phase 0, add flag - scripts/preflight.sh: Ensure can be sourced, not just executed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:50:00.154616-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:37.034619-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:37.034619-05:00","close_reason":"Integrated preflight into install.sh as Phase 0. Added run_preflight_checks() function, --skip-preflight flag, and auto-download for curl|bash scenario.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-545","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0iq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:28.766175-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-58by","title":"Refactor install_helpers.sh to remove duplicate feature-flag logic","description":"scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh currently contains duplicated/overlapping implementations for generated-vs-legacy category feature flags (multiple helpers and a later redefinition of acfs_use_generated_category). Consolidate to a single implementation, remove dead code, and add a small shell test to lock behavior.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:08:53.010083-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:56:22.523356-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:56:22.523356-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (generated category flag logic consolidated)"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5gq","title":"Task: Create command reference page","description":"# Create Command Reference Page ## Parent Feature Reference Documentation (1y8) ## Goal Create a searchable command reference page at `/learn/commands` that lists all commands installed by ACFS. ## Design ### Page Layout ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Command Reference │ │ │ │ [🔍 Search commands...] │ │ │ │ Category: [All] [Agents] [Search] [Git] [System] │ │ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ cc [Copy] │ │ │ │ Claude Code - AI coding assistant │ │ │ │ Example: cc "fix the bug in auth.ts" │ │ │ │ [Full docs →] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ cod [Copy] │ │ │ │ Codex CLI - OpenAI coding agent │ │ │ │ ... │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ... │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Command Data Structure ```typescript // lib/commands.ts interface Command { name: string; fullName: string; description: string; category: 'agents' | 'search' | 'git' | 'system' | 'stack'; example: string; docsUrl?: string; aliases?: string[]; } export const COMMANDS: Command[] = [ // Agents { name: 'cc', fullName: 'Claude Code', description: 'AI coding assistant', category: 'agents', example: 'cc "fix the bug"' }, { name: 'cod', fullName: 'Codex CLI', description: 'OpenAI coding agent', category: 'agents', example: 'cod "add tests"' }, { name: 'gmi', fullName: 'Gemini CLI', description: 'Google coding agent', category: 'agents', example: 'gmi "review code"' }, // Search { name: 'rg', fullName: 'ripgrep', description: 'Ultra-fast code search', category: 'search', example: 'rg "pattern" ./src' }, { name: 'fd', fullName: 'fd-find', description: 'Fast file finder', category: 'search', example: 'fd "*.ts"' }, { name: 'fzf', fullName: 'Fuzzy Finder', description: 'Interactive fuzzy search', category: 'search', example: 'fzf' }, // Git { name: 'lazygit', fullName: 'LazyGit', description: 'Visual git interface', category: 'git', example: 'lazygit' }, { name: 'git', fullName: 'Git', description: 'Version control', category: 'git', example: 'git status' }, // System { name: 'ntm', fullName: 'Named Tmux Manager', description: 'Session management', category: 'system', example: 'ntm new project' }, { name: 'lsd', fullName: 'LSDeluxe', description: 'Modern ls replacement', category: 'system', example: 'lsd -la' }, { name: 'bat', fullName: 'Bat', description: 'Cat with syntax highlighting', category: 'system', example: 'bat file.ts' }, { name: 'zoxide', fullName: 'Zoxide', description: 'Smart cd replacement', category: 'system', example: 'z project' }, // Stack { name: 'bd', fullName: 'Beads CLI', description: 'Task graph management', category: 'stack', example: 'bd list' }, { name: 'bv', fullName: 'Beads Viewer', description: 'Kanban task viewer', category: 'stack', example: 'bv' }, { name: 'ubs', fullName: 'Ultimate Bug Scanner', description: 'Code quality scanner', category: 'stack', example: 'ubs .' }, { name: 'cass', fullName: 'CASS', description: 'Session search', category: 'stack', example: 'cass' }, { name: 'cm', fullName: 'CASS Memory', description: 'Procedural memory', category: 'stack', example: 'cm context "auth"' }, { name: 'caam', fullName: 'CAAM', description: 'Account manager', category: 'stack', example: 'caam status' }, { name: 'am', fullName: 'Agent Mail', description: 'Agent coordination', category: 'stack', example: 'am status' }, ]; ``` ### Features 1. **Search** - Filter by command name or description 2. **Category tabs** - Quick filter by category 3. **Copy button** - One-click copy command 4. **Expandable** - Show full usage on click 5. **Deep links** - `/learn/commands#cc` jumps to command ### SEO - Title: "ACFS Command Reference" - Description: "Quick reference for all commands installed by Agent Flywheel" - Keywords: individual command names ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Page accessible at `/learn/commands` - [ ] All ACFS commands listed - [ ] Search filters results in real-time - [ ] Category tabs work - [ ] Copy button copies command - [ ] Mobile-friendly layout ## File Changes - Create: `apps/web/lib/commands.ts` - Create: `apps/web/app/learn/commands/page.tsx` - Create: `apps/web/components/command-ref-card.tsx`","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:22:20.811137-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:18.156226-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:18.156226-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented /learn/commands with search, category filters, deep links, and copyable examples.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5gq","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-1y8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:10.574895-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","title":"Add SSH key verification step (check files exist)","description":"# Task: Add SSH Key Verification Step ## Parent Epic [EPIC] SSH Key Generation Clarity ## Problem After running ssh-keygen, users don't know how to verify it worked or understand what was created. They have two files (public/private) but don't know which is which or what they're for. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/generate-ssh-key/page.tsx Add verification section after the command: \`\`\`tsx

You should see two files:

  • acfs_ed25519 — Your private key (keep this secret!)
  • acfs_ed25519.pub — Your public key (this gets shared)
 Think of it like a mailbox: the public key is your address (you share it), the private key is your mailbox key (only you have it). \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows verification command - [ ] Explains the two files and their purposes - [ ] Uses relatable analogy (mailbox) - [ ] Reassures user about what to keep secret ## Why This Matters Users who don't verify their key might not realize when something went wrong. The public/private distinction prevents confusion later when they need to share their public key.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:53:09.415672-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:53:09.415672-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","title":"Add SSH key verification step (check files exist)","description":"# Task: Add SSH Key Verification Step ## Parent Epic [EPIC] SSH Key Generation Clarity ## Problem After running ssh-keygen, users don't know how to verify it worked or understand what was created. They have two files (public/private) but don't know which is which or what they're for. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/generate-ssh-key/page.tsx Add verification section after the command: \`\`\`tsx

You should see two files:

  • acfs_ed25519 — Your private key (keep this secret!)
  • acfs_ed25519.pub — Your public key (this gets shared)
 Think of it like a mailbox: the public key is your address (you share it), the private key is your mailbox key (only you have it). \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows verification command - [ ] Explains the two files and their purposes - [ ] Uses relatable analogy (mailbox) - [ ] Reassures user about what to keep secret ## Why This Matters Users who don't verify their key might not realize when something went wrong. The public/private distinction prevents confusion later when they need to share their public key.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:53:09.415672-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:53:09.415672-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-unqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:12.253977-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","title":"EPIC: VPS Installer (install.sh)","description":"# Epic: VPS Installer (install.sh) ## Overview Build the one-liner bash installer that transforms a fresh Ubuntu VPS into a fully-armed agentic coding environment. ## Background & Reasoning This is the "magic moment" - the user pastes one command and everything happens. The installer must: - Work when run as root (common VPS default) - Work when run as ubuntu with sudo - Be idempotent (safe to run twice) - Handle connection drops and resume - Log everything for debugging ## Design Philosophy **Vibe Mode Default**: Passwordless sudo, agents with full permissions, minimal friction. **Checkpointed Phases**: Each phase writes state so reruns skip completed work. **Module Manifest**: Single source of truth (acfs.manifest.yaml) drives all installs. ## Installer Phases 1. Preflight + confirm OS (Ubuntu 24.04+ / 25.x) 2. Create/normalize `ubuntu` user + sudoers 3. Base packages (apt) 4. Shell (zsh + oh-my-zsh + p10k + plugins) 5. Dev toolchain (bun, uv, rust, go, tmux, rg, ast-grep) 6. Agent stack (claude/codex/gemini CLIs + aliases) 7. Flywheel stack (ntm, slb, ubs, cass, cm, bv, caam, mcp_agent_mail) 8. Post-install UX (onboard + doctor + summary) ## Success Criteria - [ ] Fresh Ubuntu 25.x VPS → working environment in < 15 minutes - [ ] Safe to run twice without breaking anything - [ ] All 8 Dicklesworthstone tools installed and verified - [ ] `acfs doctor` shows all green - [ ] Logs available at /var/log/acfs/install.log ## Considerations - Always prefer upstream installers when available - Verify each tool after install - Provide clear error messages with fix suggestions - Support `--dry-run` for transparency","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:06.097272-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","close_reason":"All 12 sub-tasks completed. VPS installer fully modularized with libraries for: logging, OS detection, user normalization, shell setup, CLI tools, language runtimes, coding agents, Dicklesworthstone stack, cloud/database tools, smoke test, and canonical zshrc.","labels":["epic","installer"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","title":"Create installer logging library (scripts/lib/logging.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Installer Logging Library ## Description Build a reusable logging library for consistent installer output. ## Functions ```bash log_step() # [1/8] Step description (blue) log_detail() # Indented detail (gray) log_success() # ✔ Success message (green) log_warn() # ⚠️ Warning message (yellow) log_error() # ✖ Error message (red) log_debug() # Only if ACFS_DEBUG=1 ``` ## Requirements - All output to stderr (keep stdout clean) - Support --quiet flag (suppress non-errors) - Support --verbose flag (show debug) - Timestamps in log file - ANSI color codes with fallback ## Log File Write all output to: - `/var/log/acfs/install.log` - `~/.acfs/logs/install..log` ## Implementation ```bash #!/bin/bash # scripts/lib/logging.sh ACFS_LOG_FILE="/var/log/acfs/install.log" ACFS_QUIET="${ACFS_QUIET:-0}" ACFS_VERBOSE="${ACFS_VERBOSE:-0}" log_step() { local step="$1" local msg="$2" echo -e "\033[34m[$step] $msg\033[0m" >&2 echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] STEP: $msg" >> "$ACFS_LOG_FILE" } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All log functions implemented - [ ] Colors work in terminal - [ ] --quiet suppresses output - [ ] --verbose shows debug - [ ] Log file written with timestamps - [ ] Works when sourced by other scripts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.871116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.872789-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.10","title":"Create modern CLI tools installer (scripts/lib/cli_tools.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Modern CLI Tools Installer ## Description Install modern CLI replacements that the zshrc depends on. ## Tools to Install ### Via apt (Ubuntu 24.04+) - ripgrep (rg) - fast grep - fd-find (fd) - fast find - bat/batcat - better cat - fzf - fuzzy finder - tmux - terminal multiplexer - neovim - better vim ### Via cargo (more recent versions) - lsd - modern ls with icons - dust - better du - btop - better top (or via apt) - zoxide - better cd - ast-grep (sg) - structural grep ### Via curl/installer - lazygit - git TUI - lazydocker - docker TUI - direnv - directory-specific env ## Functions ```bash install_apt_cli_tools() install_cargo_cli_tools() install_other_cli_tools() verify_cli_tools() ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All tools installed - [ ] Fallback logic (apt first, cargo if newer needed) - [ ] Verification for each tool - [ ] Idempotent","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:54.469899-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:27:23.299944-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:27:23.299944-05:00","close_reason":"Created scripts/lib/cli_tools.sh with modular installer functions for apt, cargo, and curl-based CLI tools","labels":["installer","mvp","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.10","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:54.472277-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} @@ -160,7 +160,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-gl9","title":"ShellCheck: fix SC2235 in services-setup guard prompt","description":"ShellCheck flags a subshell grouping in around the Claude Git Safety Guard prompt condition (SC2235). Replace the (..) grouping with a { ..; } block to avoid subshell and satisfy ShellCheck.","acceptance_criteria":"- In scripts/services-setup.sh line 422: if [[ "$SERVICES_SETUP_NONINTERACTIVE" != "true" ]] && ([[ -t 0 ]] || [[ -r /dev/tty ]]); then ^-- SC2235 (style): Use { ..; } instead of (..) to avoid subshell overhead. For more information: https://www.shellcheck.net/wiki/SC2235 -- Use { ..; } instead of (..) to av... passes with no SC2235","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:14:01.146149-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:14:41.801909-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:14:41.801909-05:00","close_reason":"Replaced subshell grouping with { ..; } in services-setup guard prompt condition; shellcheck passes."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-glt2","title":"Add key auth troubleshooting (fallback to password)","description":"# Task: Add Key Auth Troubleshooting ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Post-Install Transition Experience ## Problem If SSH key authentication fails when reconnecting as ubuntu, users are stuck. They need: 1. Understanding of why it might fail 2. Fallback to password authentication 3. How to fix the key setup ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/reconnect-ubuntu/page.tsx Add troubleshooting section: \`\`\`tsx
Key authentication not working?

Try with password first

Remove the -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 part and use your root password:

Then fix the key

Once connected, run the installer again to set up the key properly:

If password authentication also fails, check that your VPS is running in your provider's control panel.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Clear fallback path (remove -i flag, use password) - [ ] Instructions to fix key setup (re-run installer) - [ ] Collapsible to not clutter happy path - [ ] Final fallback (check VPS is running) ## Why This Matters Users who get stuck at reconnection have completed 90% of setup. A clear recovery path saves them from starting over.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:55:58.191747-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:55:58.191747-05:00"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-grfs","title":"Add 'What is a Terminal?' explanation section","description":"# Task: Add 'What is a Terminal?' Explanation ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Terminal Onboarding Foundation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ux1f) ## Description Add a beginner-friendly explanation of what a terminal is BEFORE asking users to install one. Currently we say "install a terminal" without explaining what it is or why they need it. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/install-terminal/page.tsx Add GuideExplain component at the top of SimplerGuide section: \`\`\`tsx A terminal is a text-based interface for controlling your computer. Instead of clicking buttons, you type commands. It might look intimidating at first, but you'll quickly get comfortable with the few commands you need.

Think of it like texting your computer: you write a short message (command), press Enter, and the computer responds. \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explanation appears before install instructions - [ ] Uses SimplerGuide/GuideExplain component for consistency - [ ] Tone is friendly and reassuring, not technical - [ ] Works well on mobile (no text overflow) ## UI/UX Notes - Keep it brief - users are eager to move forward - Use analogy (texting computer) to make it relatable - Avoid jargon like "command line interface" or "shell"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:39:21.243007-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:39:21.243007-05:00"} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-h7kw","title":"Clarify VPS provider account vs VPS instance distinction","description":"# Task: Clarify Account vs Instance Distinction ## Parent Epic [EPIC] VPS Conceptual Framework ## Problem The wizard has "Rent VPS" and "Create VPS" as separate steps. Beginners don't understand: - "Rent VPS" = Create an account with a provider (like signing up for Netflix) - "Create VPS" = Launch an actual server (like picking a show to watch) This causes confusion when they complete step 4 and think they have a VPS already. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/rent-vps/page.tsx (end of page) and create-vps/page.tsx (top) Add transition text at end of rent-vps: \`\`\`tsx Account created? Great! Now you need to actually launch a server. Having an account is like having a Netflix subscription — you still need to pick something to watch. The next step creates your actual VPS. \`\`\` Add clarification at top of create-vps: \`\`\`tsx

You have an account with your VPS provider. Now let's create the actual server (the VPS instance) that will run your development environment.

\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Clear distinction between account and instance - [ ] Relatable analogy (Netflix subscription vs picking a show) - [ ] Transition text links the two steps - [ ] Users understand they have two passwords (account + root) ## Why This Matters This confusion directly causes the "wrong password" issue at SSH connection. Users try their Hetzner account password instead of their VPS root password.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:54:03.255992-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:54:03.255992-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-h7kw","title":"Clarify VPS provider account vs VPS instance distinction","description":"# Task: Clarify Account vs Instance Distinction ## Parent Epic [EPIC] VPS Conceptual Framework ## Problem The wizard has "Rent VPS" and "Create VPS" as separate steps. Beginners don't understand: - "Rent VPS" = Create an account with a provider (like signing up for Netflix) - "Create VPS" = Launch an actual server (like picking a show to watch) This causes confusion when they complete step 4 and think they have a VPS already. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/rent-vps/page.tsx (end of page) and create-vps/page.tsx (top) Add transition text at end of rent-vps: \`\`\`tsx Account created? Great! Now you need to actually launch a server. Having an account is like having a Netflix subscription — you still need to pick something to watch. The next step creates your actual VPS. \`\`\` Add clarification at top of create-vps: \`\`\`tsx

You have an account with your VPS provider. Now let's create the actual server (the VPS instance) that will run your development environment.

\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Clear distinction between account and instance - [ ] Relatable analogy (Netflix subscription vs picking a show) - [ ] Transition text links the two steps - [ ] Users understand they have two passwords (account + root) ## Why This Matters This confusion directly causes the "wrong password" issue at SSH connection. Users try their Hetzner account password instead of their VPS root password.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:54:03.255992-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:54:03.255992-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-h7kw","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-a9nk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:17.500583-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hao","title":"[codex.3] Document OpenAI account types distinction","description":"## Goal Add clear documentation explaining the difference between OpenAI API accounts and ChatGPT accounts. ## Why This Matters Many users are confused because OpenAI has TWO distinct account/auth systems: ### 1. OpenAI API Account - **Purpose**: Programmatic API access (for developers building apps) - **Auth**: OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable - **Billing**: Pay-per-token, separate API billing - **Where**: platform.openai.com - **What uses it**: OpenAI SDK, custom applications - **NOT used by**: codex-cli ### 2. ChatGPT Account (Pro/Plus/Teams) - **Purpose**: Consumer chat interface and coding tools - **Auth**: OAuth via web browser (email/password or SSO) - **Billing**: Monthly subscription - **Where**: chat.openai.com - **What uses it**: ChatGPT website, codex-cli, mobile app - **Used by**: codex-cli! ## Documentation to Create/Update 1. Add section to README.md or create docs/agent-auth.md 2. Update acfs/onboard/lessons/04_agents_login.md with clarification 3. Consider adding to wizard website (apps/web/) ## Key Points to Document 1. codex-cli uses ChatGPT account, NOT API key 2. No OPENAI_API_KEY needed for codex-cli 3. Authentication flow: run "codex auth" → browser opens → login → done 4. May need to enable device access in ChatGPT settings 5. caam can backup/restore codex credentials just like claude/gemini ## Acceptance Criteria 1. Clear explanation of the two account types 2. Explicit statement that codex-cli uses ChatGPT, not API 3. Step-by-step auth guide for codex-cli 4. Reference to ChatGPT security settings if needed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:37.395114-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:45:34.456497-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:45:34.456497-05:00","close_reason":"Updated acfs/onboard/lessons/04_agents_login.md with clear table distinguishing ChatGPT (OAuth) vs API (key) account types. docs/codex-auth-research.md already has technical details.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hao","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ua5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:36.053709-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hby","title":"Implement AI agent updates (claude, codex, gemini)","description":"## What Implement the 'agents' category for acfs-update: - Claude Code: claude update (built-in) or reinstall via official installer - Codex CLI: bun update -g @openai/codex - Gemini CLI: bun update -g @google/gemini-cli ## Technical Details - Claude Code has native update: ~/.local/bin/claude update - If claude update fails, fall back to reinstall via installer - Codex/Gemini are bun global packages - simple bun update ## Considerations - Claude update may require re-authentication after major updates - Codex/Gemini updates are quick (just npm package updates) - Must use target user's bun, not root's ## Success Criteria - [ ] All agents update correctly - [ ] Fallback to reinstall works for Claude - [ ] Version changes logged - [ ] Authentication state preserved where possible","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:12.067763-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:14:53.146714-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:14:53.146714-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented version tracking for all agents, Claude update fallback to reinstall","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hby","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.187073-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hi7","title":"Review misc_coding_agent_tips_and_scripts and integrate relevant automation","description":"## Goal Review `https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/misc_coding_agent_tips_and_scripts` (ignore the moonlight tip) and fold the best practices into ACFS in a way that is: - ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:53:24.838062-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:43:50.225994-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:43:50.225994-05:00","close_reason":"Integrated 2 high-value tips: (1) Git Safety Guard Hook - Python PreToolUse hook blocking destructive git/filesystem commands, (2) PATH Conflict Detection - doctor.sh now warns about bun/npm shadowing native Claude installs. Treat missing bun as a recorded failure (increment FAIL_COUNT) but return 0 so the updater continues and summarizes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:37:57.713727-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:38:42.384269-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T15:38:42.384269-05:00","close_reason":"Make scripts/lib/update.sh treat missing bun as a recorded failure (FAIL_COUNT) but return 0 so remaining updates continue under set -euo pipefail.","labels":["reliability","tooling"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-n7o","title":"Docs drift: README claims install.sh runs scripts/generated but it doesn't","description":"README architecture diagram/text indicates scripts/generated installer + doctor outputs are wired into install.sh, but install.sh currently contains its own phase logic and doesn't reference scripts/generated. Decide whether to integrate install.sh with generated scripts, or update README to match reality.","notes":"Updated README to clearly state scripts/generated outputs are reference/future and are not invoked by install.sh yet (see commit e891cbe).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T19:16:14.904067-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:09:06.894046-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T20:09:06.89405-05:00","labels":["architecture","docs"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","title":"[ubu.6] Integrate upgrade phase into install.sh","description":"# Integrate Upgrade Phase into install.sh ## Purpose Wire the Ubuntu upgrade functionality into the main installer flow as "Phase -1" (before all other phases). ## Changes to install.sh ### New CLI Flags ```bash # In parse_args() --skip-ubuntu-upgrade) SKIP_UBUNTU_UPGRADE=true ;; --target-ubuntu=*) TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION="${1#*=}" ;; ``` ### New Global Variables ```bash # Ubuntu upgrade options SKIP_UBUNTU_UPGRADE=false TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION="25.10" # Default target ``` ### Integration Point Add before existing Phase 0 (preflight): ```bash main() { # ... existing setup ... # Phase -1: Ubuntu Upgrade (before everything else) if [[ "$SKIP_UBUNTU_UPGRADE" != "true" ]]; then run_ubuntu_upgrade_phase fi # Phase 0: Preflight run_preflight_checks # ... rest of phases ... } ``` ### New Function: run_ubuntu_upgrade_phase() ```bash run_ubuntu_upgrade_phase() { # Source upgrade library source "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/ubuntu_upgrade.sh" log_section "Ubuntu Version Check" # Get current version local current_version=$(ubuntu_get_version_string) log_info "Current Ubuntu version: $current_version" # Check if upgrade needed if ubuntu_version_gte "$current_version" "$TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION"; then log_success "Ubuntu $current_version meets target ($TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION)" return 0 fi # Calculate upgrade path local upgrade_path=$(ubuntu_calculate_upgrade_path "$TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION") log_info "Upgrade path: $current_version → $upgrade_path → $TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION" # Run preflight checks if ! ubuntu_preflight_checks; then log_error "Preflight checks failed. Cannot proceed with upgrade." log_info "Use --skip-ubuntu-upgrade to bypass (not recommended)" exit 1 fi # Save original args for resume after reboot export ACFS_ORIGINAL_ARGS="$*" # Initialize upgrade state state_upgrade_init "$current_version" "$TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION" "$upgrade_path" # Setup resume service ubuntu_setup_resume # Start upgrade log_step "Starting Ubuntu upgrade to $TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION" log_warn "This will take 30-60 minutes per version and require reboots." log_warn "SSH sessions will disconnect. Reconnect after reboot." ubuntu_do_upgrade # If we get here, reboot is needed ubuntu_trigger_reboot # Script exits here - resume service takes over after reboot } ``` ## Resume Detection When install.sh runs after reboot, detect if we are resuming: ```bash # At start of main() if state_upgrade_get_stage == "awaiting_reboot"; then # This is a resume - upgrade service should handle it # But if user manually runs install.sh, resume gracefully log_info "Detected in-progress upgrade. Checking status..." run_ubuntu_upgrade_phase # Will continue upgrade chain fi ``` ## Documentation Updates Update install.sh header comments with new flags: ```bash # --skip-ubuntu-upgrade Skip automatic Ubuntu version upgrade # --target-ubuntu=VER Set target Ubuntu version (default: 25.10) ``` ## Error Handling If upgrade fails: 1. Log error to /var/log/acfs/upgrade.log 2. Set error state in state.json 3. Print recovery instructions 4. Exit with non-zero code User can then: - Fix the issue manually - Re-run install.sh (will detect failed state and offer options) - Use --skip-ubuntu-upgrade to proceed on current version","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:22:11.33453-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:02:52.567131-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:02:52.567131-05:00","close_reason":"Feature fully implemented: CLI flags, run_ubuntu_upgrade_phase(), main() integration, resume detection all in place. Commit 69a4002.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-auf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:23:36.629339-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0ge","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:23:36.876802-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lhe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:43.088899-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-cxr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:43.266262-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-itb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:43.439406-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ni52","title":"Add 'Try Your First Command' interactive verification","description":"# Task: Add 'Try Your First Command' Verification ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Terminal Onboarding Foundation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ux1f) ## Description Users need a low-stakes way to verify their terminal works and practice the basic flow: type command, press Enter, see output. This builds confidence before the more complex SSH commands. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/install-terminal/page.tsx Add an interactive section with a simple command to try: \`\`\`tsx
Launch the terminal app you just installed (Ghostty, WezTerm, or Windows Terminal). You should see a blinking cursor after a symbol like $ or %. This is the "prompt" — it means the terminal is waiting for your input. The terminal should respond with: hello If you see "hello" appear, your terminal is working perfectly! You just sent a command to your computer and it responded.
\`\`\` Add a checkbox: "I ran my first command successfully" ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Clear step-by-step instructions - [ ] Simple command (echo hello) that always works - [ ] Expected output shown - [ ] Success confirmation/checkbox - [ ] Encouraging tone ("You just sent a command!") ## UI/UX Notes - This should feel like a mini-celebration, not a test - The command must work on all OS (echo works on Mac, Linux, Windows PowerShell) - Consider adding what to do if it doesn't work","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:40:03.439068-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:40:03.439068-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ni52","title":"Add 'Try Your First Command' interactive verification","description":"# Task: Add 'Try Your First Command' Verification ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Terminal Onboarding Foundation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ux1f) ## Description Users need a low-stakes way to verify their terminal works and practice the basic flow: type command, press Enter, see output. This builds confidence before the more complex SSH commands. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/install-terminal/page.tsx Add an interactive section with a simple command to try: \`\`\`tsx
Launch the terminal app you just installed (Ghostty, WezTerm, or Windows Terminal). You should see a blinking cursor after a symbol like $ or %. This is the "prompt" — it means the terminal is waiting for your input. The terminal should respond with: hello If you see "hello" appear, your terminal is working perfectly! You just sent a command to your computer and it responded.
\`\`\` Add a checkbox: "I ran my first command successfully" ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Clear step-by-step instructions - [ ] Simple command (echo hello) that always works - [ ] Expected output shown - [ ] Success confirmation/checkbox - [ ] Encouraging tone ("You just sent a command!") ## UI/UX Notes - This should feel like a mini-celebration, not a test - The command must work on all OS (echo works on Mac, Linux, Windows PowerShell) - Consider adding what to do if it doesn't work","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:40:03.439068-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:40:03.439068-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ni52","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-grfs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:06.9952-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nna","title":"Add automatic retry for transient network errors","description":"# Task: Add automatic retry for transient network errors ## Purpose Handle temporary network issues gracefully without user intervention. ## Retry Strategy ### Timing: Immediate → 5s → 15s **Rationale for these intervals:** - **Immediate retry (0s):** Many transient errors clear instantly (TCP reset, brief DNS hiccup) - **5 second wait:** Enough for most CDN/routing issues to resolve - **15 second wait:** Handles rate limiting, brief outages - **Total wait:** 20 seconds max - fast enough to not frustrate users ```bash RETRY_DELAYS=(0 5 15) # Immediate, then 5s, then 15s retry_with_backoff() { local cmd="$1" local description="$2" local max_attempts=${#RETRY_DELAYS[@]} for ((attempt=0; attempt 0)); then log_info "Retry $attempt/$((max_attempts-1)) for $description (waiting ${delay}s)..." sleep "$delay" fi if eval "$cmd" 2>/dev/null; then ((attempt > 0)) && log_info "Succeeded on retry $attempt" return 0 fi done return 1 } ``` ### Retryable Errors Only retry for transient network issues: ```bash is_retryable_error() { local exit_code="$1" local stderr="$2" # curl exit codes for network issues case "$exit_code" in 6) return 0 ;; # Could not resolve host 7) return 0 ;; # Failed to connect 28) return 0 ;; # Operation timeout 35) return 0 ;; # SSL connect error 52) return 0 ;; # Empty reply 56) return 0 ;; # Network receive error esac # Check stderr for common transient messages if [[ "$stderr" =~ (timeout|timed.out|connection.refused|temporarily) ]]; then return 0 fi return 1 # Not retryable } ``` ### Integration with fetch_and_run() ```bash verified_fetch_and_run() { local url="$1" local checksum="$2" local name="$3" shift 3 retry_with_backoff \ "verify_checksum \"$url\" \"$checksum\" \"$name\"" \ "fetching $name" local status=$? if ((status \!= 0)); then log_error "Failed to fetch $name after ${#RETRY_DELAYS[@]} attempts" return 1 fi # Content already verified, now run verified_content | bash -s -- "$@" } ``` ## Non-Retryable Scenarios - HTTP 404 (script doesnt exist) - HTTP 403 (permission denied) - Checksum mismatch (not a network issue) - Script execution errors ## Dependencies - Used by: security.sh fetch functions - Used by: install.sh download operations ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Retry delays: 0s, 5s, 15s (total 20s max) - [ ] Only retries network-related curl errors - [ ] Clear logging of retry attempts - [ ] Does not retry checksum mismatches - [ ] Does not retry HTTP 4xx errors","notes":"Implementing retry-with-backoff for transient curl failures in scripts/lib/security.sh (0s/5s/15s).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:50:37.817313-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:02:52.336748-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:02:52.336748-05:00","close_reason":"Added retry-with-backoff (0/5/15s) for transient curl failures. Implemented acfs_curl_with_retry_and_sentinel() in scripts/lib/security.sh and wired into fetch_checksum()/verify_checksum(). Added the same retry logic to install.sh's acfs_fetch_url_content() and used it for remote checksums.yaml fetch.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nna","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:28.919648-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-o85","title":"Task: Port agent lessons (4-6) to webapp","description":"# Port Agent Lessons (4-6) to Webapp ## Parent Feature Lesson Content Pages (cxk) ## Scope Create lesson pages for the agent-focused lessons: - Lesson 4: Agent Commands (`04_agents_login.md`) - Lesson 5: NTM Command Center (`05_ntm_core.md`) - Lesson 6: NTM Prompt Palette (`06_ntm_command_palette.md`) ## Why Grouped These lessons are interconnected: - All focus on using the AI coding agents - Build on each other (auth → orchestration → prompts) - Share visual elements (command tables, prompt examples) ## Lesson-Specific Enhancements ### Lesson 4: Agent Commands This is a critical lesson teaching users to authenticate with agents. Enhancements: - Interactive auth status checker component - Clear distinction between ChatGPT OAuth vs API keys - CAAM backup/restore workflow diagram - Warning callouts for common mistakes Key Content: ```markdown | Agent | Command | Alias | Company | |-------|---------|-------|---------| | Claude Code | `claude` | `cc` | Anthropic | | Codex CLI | `codex` | `cod` | OpenAI | | Gemini CLI | `gemini` | `gmi` | Google | ``` ### Lesson 5: NTM Command Center Introduces the core orchestration tool. Enhancements: - Visual session management diagram - `ntm spawn` command builder (optional interactive) - Session lifecycle visualization Key Content: - Session types and naming - Spawning agents - Attaching and detaching - Sending prompts ### Lesson 6: NTM Prompt Palette Teaches pre-built prompts and customization. Enhancements: - Searchable prompt gallery - Copy-to-clipboard for prompts - Category filtering (research, coding, review) ## Visual Components Needed 1. **AgentCard** - Shows agent status (authenticated/not) 2. **PromptCard** - Copyable prompt with syntax highlighting 3. **SessionDiagram** - Visual representation of ntm sessions ## Cross-Links - Link to wizard Step 7 (Set Up Accounts) for auth setup - Link to Reference Docs for command details - Link to Lesson 7 (Flywheel) for full workflow ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 3 agent lessons accessible - [ ] Agent tables render correctly - [ ] Command examples have copy buttons - [ ] Warning callouts display prominently - [ ] Prompt examples are copyable - [ ] Mobile-friendly layout ## Dependencies Same as foundation lessons: - Lesson progress tracking (ori) - MDX rendering pipeline (25i) - Lesson layout and navigation (j9a)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:21:18.624349-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.584372-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.584372-05:00","close_reason":"All 9 lessons already load and render from source markdown files via lesson-content.tsx. No separate porting needed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-o85","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ori","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:09.339153-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-o85","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-25i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:09.549317-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-o85","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-j9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:09.760014-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-oaoq","title":"Update wizard OS guidance to Ubuntu 25.10 (auto-upgrade)","description":"Wizard pages still reference Ubuntu 24.04.
